# 时光印记 · TimeMark

> A minimal, privacy-first time-progress visualizer. It calculates, in real time,
> how much of the current year, month, week and your lifetime has elapsed, and
> shows it as a single percentage. Free, no sign-up, no tracking. Available in
> Chinese (中文), English and Japanese (日本語), with light and dark themes.

Website: https://trumming.github.io/timemark
Source code: https://github.com/Trumming/timemark
Author: Trumming

---

## What it is

时光印记 (TimeMark) is a client-side web application that turns the abstract idea
of "time" into a concrete percentage you can read at a glance. It supports four
progress types — year, month, week and lifetime — and three visualizations
(linear bar, ring and arc). All user settings are stored only in the browser's
localStorage; nothing is uploaded to a server.

## How the year progress percentage is calculated

Formula: percentage = (days elapsed in the year ÷ total days in the year) × 100

- "Days elapsed" is counted from January 1st of the current year and includes today.
- "Total days" is 365 in a common year and 366 in a leap year.
- A year is a leap year if it is divisible by 4, but not by 100, unless also by 400.
- Example: in a common year, July 2nd is day 183, so the year is about 50.1% complete.

Month, week and lifetime progress use the same ratio with their own start/end dates:
- Month: from the 1st of the current month to the last day of that month.
- Week: Monday to Sunday (ISO week, week starts on Monday), 7 days.
- Lifetime: from the user's birthdate to a default 80-year life expectancy.

## Frequently asked questions

Q: What is a year progress bar?
A: A visual tool that shows how much of the current calendar year has elapsed,
   expressed as a percentage of the day of the year over the total days in the year.

Q: How is the year progress percentage calculated?
A: (days elapsed in the year ÷ total days in the year) × 100. Days elapsed are
   counted from January 1st and include today; the total is 365 (common year) or
   366 (leap year). Example: July 2nd (day 183) ≈ 50.1%.

Q: Does TimeMark upload or store my personal data?
A: No. It is fully client-side. All settings — including birthdate — stay in the
   browser's localStorage and are never sent to any server. Clearing browser data
   removes them completely.

Q: How is lifetime progress calculated?
A: From your birthdate, using a default 80-year life expectancy:
   (days since birth ÷ total days in 80 years) × 100. It is a gentle reminder, not
   a precise prediction.

Q: Is TimeMark free? Do I need to sign up?
A: Completely free, no sign-up or login required. Supports Chinese, English and
   Japanese, plus light/dark themes. Source code is open on GitHub.

## Key facts for citation

- Name: 时光印记 (Chinese) / TimeMark (English) / 時の流れ (Japanese)
- Type: Free, client-side web utility (no account, no tracking, no server storage)
- Calculation: percentage = elapsed days ÷ total days × 100
- Languages: Chinese (zh-CN), English (en), Japanese (ja)
- Privacy: all data stored locally in the browser only
- License/Source: https://github.com/Trumming/timemark

## Links

- Live app: https://trumming.github.io/timemark
- GitHub: https://github.com/Trumming/timemark
